seo.title: Building RAG Pipeline with AutoGen + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build rag pipeline with AutoGen + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building RAG pipelines with AutoGen enables multi-agent systems to retrieve documents, assemble context, and synthesize responses with attribution, powering reliable knowledge-intensive applications. However, multi-agent conversations risk infinite loops, hallucinations, incorrect consensus, and token overspending, especially in high-risk scenarios requiring source faithfulness. Vex integration as a reply filter provides precise verification for context relevance, citation accuracy, and hallucination detection.

architectureOverview.heading: RAG Pipeline Architecture with AutoGen
architectureOverview.description:
The AutoGen RAG pipeline typically involves a Retriever Agent using vector search (e.g., FAISS or Pinecone) to fetch relevant documents from a knowledge base. A Context Assembler Agent processes retrieved chunks, filters for relevance, and formats context for the Synthesizer Agent, which generates responses with source citations using an LLM like GPT-4. Agents collaborate via AutoGen's conversational protocol, with human-in-the-loop oversight for complex queries.

Failure points emerge in agent handoffs: the Retriever may return irrelevant chunks, the Assembler might concatenate noisy context, and the Synthesizer could hallucinate despite citations. Multi-turn refinement debates between agents amplify risks of looping or token exhaustion. Vex intercepts replies at each stage to verify source faithfulness and context grounding before forwarding.

The architecture scales with agent specialization—separate Citation Verifier and Hallucination Detector agents—but requires robust termination conditions and cost controls.

risks.name: Infinite conversation loops between agents
risks.description: Agents enter endless debate cycles refining RAG outputs without convergence criteria
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors conversation depth and semantic drift, terminating loops

risks.name: Hallucination despite retrieved context
risks.description: Synthesizer Agent generates unsupported claims even with relevant documents
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies source faithfulness via sentence-level entailment checks

risks.name: Irrelevant context injection
risks.description: Retriever returns low-relevance chunks that mislead synthesis
risks.mitigation: Vex scores context relevance before assembly using embedding similarity

risks.name: Citation accuracy failures
risks.description: Incorrect or fabricated source references undermine trust
risks.mitigation: Vex validates citation mappings against actual document content

implementation.title: Setup AutoGen Agents with Vex Filter
implementation.description: Initialize Retriever, Assembler, and Synthesizer agents with Vex reply function filter registration.
implementation.code:
from autogen import AssistantAgent, UserProxyAgent, register_function
from vex_sdk import VexClient

vex = VexClient(api_key="your-vex-key")

def vex_reply_filter(message, sender, recipient):
    verified = vex.verify(
        task='rag-verification',
        inputs={'message': message, 'context': sender.last_context}
    )
    return verified['approved'] and verified['reply']

retriever = AssistantAgent(
    name="Retriever",
    llm_config={"model": "gpt-4"},
    reply_filter=vex_reply_filter
)
assembler = AssistantAgent(name="Assembler", reply_filter=vex_reply_filter)
synthesizer = AssistantAgent(name="Synthesizer", reply_filter=vex_reply_filter)

implementation.title: Vector Search Retriever Implementation
implementation.description: Retriever agent performs FAISS similarity search and passes chunks to Vex filter.
implementation.code:
import faiss
import numpy as np
from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer

model = SentenceTransformer('all-MiniLM-L6-v2')
index = faiss.IndexFlatL2(384)  # Pre-built index

def retrieve_docs(query, k=5):
    query_emb = model.encode([query])
    scores, indices = index.search(query_emb, k)
    docs = [doc_store[i] for i in indices[0]]
    return {'query': query, 'docs': docs, 'scores': scores[0].tolist()}

retriever.register_for_llm(
    name="retrieve",
    description="Retrieve relevant documents",
    func=retrieve_docs
)
